'''Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''taka no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''taka kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the northern part of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}. Also called Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko (多珂国造).

== Overview ==
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' and the ''{{ill|Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|ja|常陸国風土記}}'' it is written '''Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (多珂国造), and in the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' it is written '''Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (高国造).

=== Ancestry ===
* The ''Kojiki'' names {{ill|Take-Hiratori-no-Mikoto|ja|武夷鳥命|lt=Take-Hiratori-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=建比良鳥命}}, son of {{ill|Amenohohi|ja|天之菩卑能命}}, as the distant ancestor.
* The "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' states that Misahi-no-Mikoto, grandson of Mitsurogi-no-Mikoto, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}}.
* According to the ''Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki'', {{ill|Takemisahi-no-Mikoto|ja|建御狭日命}} is said to have taken office as Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|lt=Emperor Seimu|lt_ja=成務朝}}.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Iwaki clan|ja|岩城氏|lt=Iwaki clan|lt_ja=石城氏}}''' (''iwaki-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (カバネ)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}}), an early branch clan of the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造|lt=Izumo-no-Omi|lt_ja=出雲臣}}.

== Base ==
=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then called '''Taka Province''' (高国), corresponding to the later '''{{ill|Taka District|ja|多賀郡|lt=Taka District|lt_ja=多珂郡}}''' of {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}} — present-day {{ill|Hitachi, Ibaraki|ja|日立市|lt=Hitachi City|lt_ja=日立市}}, {{ill|Takahagi, Ibaraki|ja|高萩市|lt=Takahagi City|lt_ja=高萩市}}, {{ill|Kita-Ibaraki, Ibaraki|ja|北茨城市|lt=Kita-Ibaraki City|lt_ja=北茨城市}}, and so on in {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
Possibly {{ill|Sawawa-chigi Shrine|ja|佐波波地祇神社}} (Kita-Ibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture).

== Descendants ==
'''Iwaki-no-Miyabe''' (''iwaki-no-miyabe''; Taka-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko Iwaki-no-Atai Miyabe)
*: A {{ill|gōzoku|ja|豪族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}} and Taka no Kuni no Miyatsuko. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Kōtoku|ja|孝徳天皇|lt=Emperor Kōtoku|lt_ja=孝徳朝}}, he petitioned with Iwaki-no-Kōri-no-Miyatsukobe Shikoaka to divide Taka Province into Taka District and Iwaki District.

== See also ==
*{{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

== References ==
*''Kokushi Daijiten'' ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}})
*Sakamoto Tarō, Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan)
*''Nihon Kodai Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan)
*''Nihon-shi Kō-jiten'' ({{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}})
*''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoten)
*''Nihon-shi Sōran'' ({{ill|Shinjinbutsu Ōraisha|ja|新人物往来社}})

{{DEFAULTSORT:たかのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]]
[[Category:常陸国]]
[[Category:茨城県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:高国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
